name: ruptures (Change Point Detection)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-2-Clause); Charles Truong / ENS Paris-Saclay

performance.notes: Cython backend; PELT is O(n) for fixed complexity; dynamic programming algorithms

agent_readiness.notes: Change point detection in time series. import ruptures as rpt. algo = rpt.Pelt(model='rbf').fit(signal). result = algo.predict(pen=3). # breakpoints list. rpt.show.display(signal, result). Algorithms: Pelt (O(n)), Binseg (O(n log n)), BottomUp, Window, Dynp. Models: 'l1', 'l2', 'rbf', 'linear', 'normal', 'ar'. Number of breaks known: algo.predict(n_bkps=5). Penalty selection: rpt.KernelCPD(kernel='rbf').fit(signal).predict(pen=...). Multi-dimensional signal: signal shape (n_samples, n_features). Metrics: rpt.metrics.precision_recall(true_bkps, pred_bkps). Simulation: rpt.pw_constant(n_samples, n_features, n_bkps).

gotchas: Penalty parameter pen requires tuning — no universal default
PELT assumes one cost model — model mismatch leads to missed or false breakpoints
Multivariate signals require choosing appropriate model (rbf, linear, ar)
No streaming/online changepoint detection — batch only
best_when: Offline changepoint detection in time series: when did the signal change?
Segmenting ECG, accelerometer, financial, sensor data into homogeneous regions
When number of changepoints is unknown (PELT with penalty) or known (Dynp)
avoid_when: Online/streaming changepoint detection (use BOCD or CUSUM)
Anomaly detection for isolated points (use STUMPY or PyOD)
summary: ruptures detects change points in time series. rpt.Pelt(model='rbf').fit(signal).predict(pen=3). Algorithms: PELT (O(n)), Binseg, BottomUp, Window, Dynp. Models: l1, l2, rbf, linear, normal, ar. n_bkps for known count. Multivariate. Visualization. BSD-2 by Charles Truong/ENS. Best for offline time series segmentation. Penalty pen requires tuning. Batch only — no streaming. Model selection matters for accuracy.
